w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: Final Project: Java OOP Desktop Application (GUI-Based)
Provide a clear and descriptive title that reflects the system’s function.

This project aims to apply Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) principles in Java through the development of a Graphical User Interface (GUI) application using WindowBuilder. Students will integrate design, functionality, and user interaction into a complete desktop system.
Students must develop a Java desktop application with GUI using WindowBuilder (Swing). The system should solve a real-world problem or simulate a functional process.
Be built using WindowBuilder (Eclipse Swing Designer)
Include a user-friendly GUI (forms, buttons, labels, text fields, etc.)
Apply OOP principles (encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction)
Use multiple classes and proper object relationships
Accept user input through GUI components
Display results dynamically in the interface
Follow proper coding structure and naming convention
This rubric will be used to evaluate your final project. Review
Ad hoc solution; program “designed at the keyboard
Program produces correct output half of the time
Program does not compile or run at all
Program satisfies specification completely and correctly
Many parts of the specification not implemented
Passing is a raw score of 60, ZERO BASED
Code hard to follow in one reading; poor use of language capabilities
Incomprehensive code, appropriate language capabilities not used
Partial, poorly written or poorly formatted comments
Programs that usefully extend the requirements
Programs that use a particularly good algorithm
Program that are particularly well written or use the capabilities of the
